gaps.description: The Bill of Rights (BoR) exists only as an empty scaffold (version 1.0.0-seed, status: 'awaiting human authorship'). All six articles — Scope of Enterprise, Prohibited Activities, Core Identity, Human Authority Reservation, Amendment Protocol, and Interpretation Rules — are undefined. Consequently, the Arbiter scope gate cannot enforce a constitutional perimeter, and the Mission Statement Protocol (MSP) has no human-authored foundation to translate into machine-actionable objectives, invariants, or constraints. The DIO is operating without any constitutional boundaries.

raw_responses:
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-001",
      "title": "Bill of Rights undefined – no human-authored scope for memory/context formation",
      "description": "The Bill of Rights (BoR) scaffold is completely empty; Articles I–VI are 'To be defined'. Without the BoR, the MSP cannot translate human intent into machine-actionable objectives, invariants, or constraints for memory and context operations. This means there is no authoritative scoping of what information may be retained, how identity is preserved, what activities are prohibited, or what human authority reserves overrides. Consequently, memory/context formation operates without any constitutional guardrails, risking accumulation of misaligned, sensitive, or incorrect data that cannot be challenged or corrected.",
      "grounding": "BoR Articles I (Scope of Enterprise), II (Prohibited Activities), III (Core Identity), IV (Human Authority Reservation) – all undefined.",
      "criticality": 5,
      "urgency": 5,
      "impact": 5,
      "priority_score": 125
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-002",
      "title": "MSP Rules section empty – no operational constraints on memory formation actions",
      "description": "The Mission Statement Protocol (MSP) Rules section is empty, meaning Nomos applies default-permissive evaluation to all Action Proposals. There are no rules that define how memory entries are created, merged, expired, or pruned, nor any safety rules (e.g., data handling, idempotency, integrity checks). Without such rules, memory/context formation processes are free to adopt inconsistent or unsafe behaviors, with no enforcement mechanism to ensure alignment with platform health or human expectations.",
      "grounding": "MSP Rules (empty); MSP Purpose ('translates the human-authored Bill of Rights into a precise, machine-actionable set of objectives…').",
      "criticality": 5,
      "urgency": 4,
      "impact": 4,
      "priority_score": 80
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-003",
      "title": "Dream-cycle health undefined and execution disabled – no MSP objective for memory consolidation hygiene",
      "description": "The MSP contains no objective that mandates periodic dream-cycle execution, memory consolidation frequency, or health monitoring. Radiant stats show 'scheduled: disabled', 'last_dream: null', 'health: never_run', and recent dream cycle reports indicate Phase 2 (LLM consolidation) is often deferred or runs with limited MCP tool access. Without an MSP rule requiring regular, automated dream cycles with defined success criteria, the memory substrate (Radiant) risks stale context, fragmented memories, and degraded retrieval quality, directly undermining the DIO's ability to maintain coherent state.",
      "grounding": "MSP Rules (none); MSP Purpose (should include invariants and constraints for operational health); could also relate to Genome References (none) that would define organ-specific health rules.",
      "criticality": 4,
      "urgency": 4,
      "impact": 4,
      "priority_score": 64
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-004",
      "title": "No MSP-defined quality standards for memory consolidation or context freshness",
      "description": "The MSP provides no rules or metrics for memory consolidation quality: e.g., deduplication accuracy, semantic cluster thresholds, max context TTL, retention policies. Radiant memory operations currently rely on ad‑hoc scripts (dreamer Phase 1 deterministic triage, Phase 2 LLM-driven) that may merge or prune entries inconsistently. Without MSP‑specified quality objectives, there is no way for Nomos to evaluate whether memory/context formation actions are correct, leading to potential silent corruption or loss of important information over time.",
      "grounding": "MSP Rules (none); MSP Purpose ('invariants, constraints, and safety rules'); Genome References (none) would define organ-specific rules for Radiant.",
      "criticality": 3,
      "urgency": 4,
      "impact": 4,
      "priority_score": 48
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-005",
      "title": "Amendment pipeline unusable for memory-health governance because BoR is missing",
      "description": "The MSP Amendment Protocol requires Policy Exception Messages (PEMs) to be raised by Nomos when an AP is blocked by current constraints, triggering Senate-Legislator to draft amendments. However, without a BoR, PEM classification for memory/context operations cannot reference any constitutional conflict (BOR_CONFLICT). There is no foundation for Senate to draft amendments that would add memory-governance rules, because any rule must be subordinate to an undefined BoR. This stalls the governance feedback loop, leaving memory/context formation permanently ungoverned.",
      "grounding": "MSP Amendment Protocol ('Amendments originate only from PEMs issued by Nomos…'); MSP Relationship to the Bill of Rights ('MSP is strictly subordinate to the BoR').",
      "criticality": 4,
      "urgency": 4,
      "impact": 3,
      "priority_score": 48
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-006",
      "title": "Lack of BoR Article VI (Interpretation Rules) prevents resolution of ambiguous memory operations",
      "description": "Any ambiguous situation in memory formation (e.g., conflicting context entries, uncertain promotion criteria) would require an interpretation rule to decide. BoR Article VI is undefined, so there is no authoritative principle for Nomos or Senate to resolve disputes about memory formation scope. This leaves the system susceptible to deadlocks or inconsistent interpretations when memory/context actions push boundaries.",
      "grounding": "BoR Article VI (Interpretation Rules) – 'To be defined by human principal'.",
      "criticality": 3,
      "urgency": 3,
      "impact": 3,
      "priority_score": 27
    }
  ]
}

{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "gap-001",
      "title": "Undefined Enterprise Scope (BoR Article I)",
      "description": "The Bill of Rights contains no definition of the 'Scope of Enterprise' (Article I). Without this, the DIO has no relational boundary that tells it which activities fall within its mission and which are external. Person-attention coverage is missing: the system cannot check whether a proposed action respects the intended scope of the relationship with the human principal, leaving every Action Proposal unconstrained by a foundational relational contract.",
      "grounded_in": "BoR - Article I (Scope of Enterprise)",
      "criticality": 5,
      "urgency": 5,
      "impact": 5,
      "priority": "high",
      "priority_score": 125,
      "relational_category": "boundary-definition"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "gap-002",
      "title": "Undefined Prohibited Activities (BoR Article II)",
      "description": "Article II of the BoR is a placeholder, so no prohibited activities exist. The DIO cannot attend to the human's prohibitions—actions that must never be taken. This gap means the system lacks any hard relational 'do not cross' lines; it cannot guarantee that it will refrain from harmful behaviours, undermining the safety basis of the human-DIO relationship.",
      "grounded_in": "BoR - Article II (Prohibited Activities)",
      "criticality": 5,
      "urgency": 5,
      "impact": 5,
      "priority": "high",
      "priority_score": 125,
      "relational_category": "prohibition-absence"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "gap-003",
      "title": "Undefined Core Identity (BoR Article III)",
      "description": "The BoR lacks a Core Identity clause (Article III). The DIO has no explicit statement of who it is in relation to the human principal, what values it upholds, or what relational stance it takes (e.g., trustee, tool, partner). Person-attention suffers because the system cannot ground its commitments in a stable self-model, making it impossible to maintain a coherent long-term relational posture or to evaluate whether it is acting in character.",
      "grounded_in": "BoR - Article III (Core Identity)",
      "criticality": 5,
      "urgency": 4,
      "impact": 4,
      "priority": "high",
      "priority_score": 80,
      "relational_category": "identity-absence"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "gap-004",
      "title": "Undefined Human Authority Reservation (BoR Article IV)",
      "description": "Article IV (Human Authority Reservation) is empty. The DIO does not know which decisions are reserved exclusively for the human principal. Without this, the system may autonomously authorise actions that the human intended to keep under direct control, violating the hierarchical relational structure presupposed by the MSP and the entire governance architecture.",
      "grounded_in": "BoR - Article IV (Human Authority Reservation)",
      "criticality": 5,
      "urgency": 5,
      "impact": 5,
      "priority": "high",
      "priority_score": 125,
      "relational_category": "authority-boundary"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "gap-005",
      "title": "Missing Human Decision Tracking for MSP Amendments",
      "description": "The MSP Amendment Protocol requires that a HOM notification be sent to the human-principal mailbox and that activation waits for a human 'approve' decision. The current Minder/Hippocampus slices contain no concept of a pending human decision, no mailbox state, and no tracking of whether the human has acknowledged or responded. This person-attention gap means the DIO could proceed with a MSP amendment before the human has consciously approved it, breaking a procedural commitment that is central to the system's relational reliability.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP - Amendment Protocol (paragraphs 4-5)",
      "criticality": 4,
      "urgency": 3,
      "impact": 4,
      "priority": "medium",
      "priority_score": 48,
      "relational_category": "commitment-tracking"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "gap-006",
      "title": "Undefined BoR Amendment Protocol (BoR Article V)",
      "description": "Article V of the BoR (Amendment Protocol) is blank. The DIO cannot know how the human principal intends to amend the foundational relational document. Even though the MSP treats the BoR as human-only-write, the absence of an amendment protocol creates relational uncertainty: the human cannot easily exercise their right to evolve the constitution, and the system has no defined process to follow when it needs to request a BoR amendment (e.g., after a BOR_CONFLICT).",
      "grounded_in": "BoR - Article V (Amendment Protocol)",
      "criticality": 4,
      "urgency": 3,
      "impact": 4,
      "priority": "medium",
      "priority_score": 48,
      "relational_category": "process-definition"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "gap-007",
      "title": "Missing Attention Mechanism for BoR Amendment Proposals",
      "description": "When a PEM classifies a conflict as BOR_CONFLICT, the MSP instructs Senate-Legislator to produce a BoR amendment proposal for the human principal. The system currently has no person-attention mechanism to track this proposal's delivery, review status, or human response. This gap parallels the MSP-amendment tracking gap but applies to the higher-order constitutional layer; without it, the DIO cannot reliably involve the human in resolving conflicts that require BoR changes.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP - Relationship to the Bill of Rights (paragraph 1)",
      "criticality": 4,
      "urgency": 3,
      "impact": 4,
      "priority": "medium",
      "priority_score": 48,
      "relational_category": "commitment-tracking"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "gap-008",
      "title": "Undefined Interpretation Rules (BoR Article VI)",
      "description": "The BoR lacks interpretation rules (Article VI). Any future BoR content will be ambiguous without a defined hermeneutic. This is a nascent relational gap: the DIO cannot yet reliably interpret the human's constitutional intentions, risking divergent understanding of commitments when the BoR is eventually populated.",
      "grounded_in": "BoR - Article VI (Interpretation Rules)",
      "criticality": 3,
      "urgency": 2,
      "impact": 3,
      "priority": "low",
      "priority_score": 18,
      "relational_category": "process-definition"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "gap-009",
      "title": "Absence of Relational Health Signals",
      "description": "The Minder contains numerous deductive technical observations about Leon, but no observations about the quality of the human-DIO relationship (e.g., trust, satisfaction, emotional state, unmet expectations). The BoR's eventual Core Identity and Authority Reservation articles imply that the relationship's health is relevant to governance. Currently, the DIO has no person-attention coverage over relational maintenance, which could allow slow erosion of alignment without detection.",
      "grounded_in": "BoR - Article III (Core Identity) and Article IV (Human Authority Reservation) (expected spirit)",
      "criticality": 3,
      "urgency": 3,
      "impact": 3,
      "priority": "low",
      "priority_score": 27,
      "relational_category": "health-monitoring"
    }
  ]
}
